summary: Implemented M3 tribute and troop obligation settlement through explicit vassalage-sourced state, versioned command payloads, deterministic accounting, M2 cash/grain resource draws, M3 troop commitment placeholders, refusal/default audit trails, and shared player/AI validation paths.

tests_run.result: Modified protocol/sim-core/tests/project state files; untracked outbox handoff and tests/sim-core-m3-tribute-troop-obligations.test.ts.
Initial TDD run failed before implementation: 6 tests failed against missing accounting/source/resource/duplicate-settlement behavior.
13 test files passed; 81 tests passed. Node emitted existing MODULE_TYPELESS_PACKAGE_JSON warning for tests/fixtures/deterministic-rng-worker.ts.
Node/worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd; save/load byte length 10920.
Architecture boundaries passed; determinism passed; root Vitest 23 files/140 tests passed; web Vitest 1 file/3 tests passed.

tests_run.command: node -e JSON parse/shape check for M3-TRIBUTE-TROOP-OBLIGATIONS-001

decisions: R3 protocol/world-state command payload change: create-obligation now requires explicit obligationCategory and vassalage obligationSource; settlement now requires actionKind, dueDay, reasonCode, executorCharacterId, and officeId.
M3 troop obligations remain headcount/commitment accounting only via m3-troop-commitment-placeholder source movements; no M4 armies, marching, campaign movement, or combat state was introduced.
Cash/grain tribute fulfillment draws deterministically from debtor source districts in M2 population groups; no source district means structured obligation-resource-insufficient rejection.
Illegal obligation creation requires debtor to be direct vassal of creditor, preventing double-counting across a suzerain chain.
Current M3 save boundary remains unsupported by requestSaveV1; tests assert the existing structured refusal instead of changing save-format.
risks: Domain event kind remains sim.obligation-fulfilled with actionKind differentiating fulfillment, refusal, remission, pursuit, and default-breach; systems_architect should confirm whether a distinct sim.obligation-settled event kind is preferred before acceptance.
M3 save-format support is intentionally not implemented because packages/save-format is forbidden for this task; M3 obligation state still cannot be emitted in save bytes.
Reliability explanations are M3 scaffolds derived from administrative burden and executor character context; no hidden reputation or full diplomacy consequences are added.
requested_action: Review R3 protocol/world-state shape, deterministic accounting, no-M4 boundary, and whether actionKind on sim.obligation-fulfilled is acceptable for refusal/default events before routing onward.
